sections.paragraph:
ὄγκος1

the barb of an arrow, in pl. the barbed points, Il.


Etym. From same Root as Lat. uncus.

ὄγκος2

I. bulk, size, mass, Lat. moles, Plat., etc.

2. a bulk, mass, heap, ὄ. φρυγάνων a heap of fagots, Hdt.; σμικρὸς ὄ. ἐν σμικρῷ κύτει, of a dead man's ashes, Soph.; ὅ. γαστρός, of a child in the womb, Eur.

II. metaph. weight, trouble, Soph.

2. weight, importance, dignity, pride, and in bad sense self-importance, pretension, Soph., Eur., etc.


Etym. From Root ΕΓΚ, in ἐνεγκεῖν to bear.
